scenarios.description: When the token iterator has no tokens (loop-0), parse should call startDocument/endDocument without invoking handleAtRule or handleRuleSet
scenarios.precondition: Stub CssParser.scan(...) to return an iterator with hasNext() == false; no mocks besides scan stub
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument() and doc.endDocument() are each called exactly once; no calls to handleAtRule or handleRuleSet
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->emptyIterator(); Reader r; CssErrorHandler err; CssContentHandler doc;
assert doc.startDocument() called once; assert doc.endDocument() called once;

scenarios.description: When the iterator yields one ATKEYWORD token (loop-1, branch-true), parse should invoke handleAtRule once then endDocument
scenarios.precondition: Stub CssParser.scan(...) to return iterator with one CssToken of type ATKEYWORD; stub handleAtRule to record invocation; no other mocks
scenarios.expectedResult: handleAtRule(startToken, iterator, doc, err) is called once; doc.startDocument() and doc.endDocument() called once
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->iterator([ATKEYWORD]); stub handleAtRule to record; Reader r; err; doc;
assert handleAtRule called once; assert doc.endDocument() called

scenarios.description: When the iterator yields one non-ATKEYWORD token (loop-1, branch-false), parse should invoke handleRuleSet once then endDocument
scenarios.precondition: Stub CssParser.scan(...) to return iterator with one CssToken of type IDENT; stub handleRuleSet to record invocation
scenarios.expectedResult: handleRuleSet(startToken, iterator, doc, err) is called once; doc.startDocument() and doc.endDocument() called once
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->iterator([IDENT]); stub handleRuleSet to record; Reader r; err; doc;
assert handleRuleSet called once; assert doc.endDocument() called

scenarios.description: When the iterator yields ATKEYWORD then IDENT tokens (loop-N, mix of branches), parse should call handleAtRule then handleRuleSet
scenarios.precondition: Stub CssParser.scan(...) to return iterator([ATKEYWORD, IDENT]); stub both handleAtRule and handleRuleSet to record invocations
scenarios.expectedResult: handleAtRule called once then handleRuleSet called once; doc.startDocument() and doc.endDocument() each once
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->iterator([ATKEYWORD, IDENT]); stub both handlers; Reader r; err; doc;
assert handleAtRule count==1; assert handleRuleSet count==1

scenarios.description: When scan(...) throws IOException, parse should propagate the IOException
scenarios.precondition: Stub CssParser.scan(...) to throw IOException("fail-scan"); no other stubs
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->throw IOException("fail-scan"); Reader r; err; doc;

scenarios.description: When scan(...) throws CssException, parse should propagate the CssException
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->throw new CssException("fail-css"); Reader r; err; doc;

scenarios.description: When handleAtRule throws PrematureEOFException, parse should break loop and still endDocument (exception suppressed)
scenarios.precondition: Stub CssParser.scan(...) to return iterator([ATKEYWORD]); stub handleAtRule to throw PrematureEOFException() to simulate premature EOF
scenarios.expectedResult: doc.startDocument() and doc.endDocument() called once; no exception propagated
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->iterator([ATKEYWORD]); stub handleAtRule->throw PrematureEOFException;

scenarios.description: When handleRuleSet throws CssGrammarException, parse should propagate that CssException
scenarios.precondition: Stub CssParser.scan(...) to return iterator([IDENT]); stub handleRuleSet to throw new CssGrammarException(GRAMMAR_UNEXPECTED_TOKEN, loc, locale, "tok");
scenarios.expectedResult: CssGrammarException is thrown with code GRAMMAR_UNEXPECTED_TOKEN
pseudocode: Parser p stubbed scan->iterator([IDENT]); stub handleRuleSet->throw CssGrammarException(GRAMMAR_UNEXPECTED_TOKEN,...);
throws CssGrammarException with error code GRAMMAR_UNEXPECTED_TOKEN
